growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Wecker, Patricia et al. Transcriptional response of the model planctomycete Rhodopirellula baltica SH1T to changing environmental conditions. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-410. [PMC2754498]
growth_records.snippet: cells were grown as chemostat cultures in a mineral medium containing 10 mM glucose as the sole carbon source and 1 mM ammonium chloride as a nitrogen source at 28C. Chemostat ... parameters used were: pH 7.4, average dilution rate 0.75 ml/
growth_records.notes: Aerobic glucose-limited chemostat steady state. Dilution rate reported as volumetric flow 0.75 mL/min into a 1 L (1000 mL) chemostat. D = 0.75 mL/min x 60 min/h / 1000 mL = 0.045 h-1. At steady state mu = D = 0.045 h-1 (calculated). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.045 = 15.4 h (calculated). NOTE: an unrelated batch-optimal doubling time of 10-12 h is quoted in-text but is cited from ref [21] (not measured here) and reflects unconstrained optimal growth, not this glucose-limited chemostat. No substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates are reported (transcriptomics/microarray study), so uptake_rates/secretion_rates are empty. Only glucose (10 mM) and NH4Cl (1 mM) concentrations are stated in text; full mineral-medium base is referenced to [20].
